dialogueChunks.intro: Look at the fields, {player_name}. Nothing has grown here in weeks. The soil itself rejects every seed we plant.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It started when a work crew clearing land near {location} knocked over an old shrine {location_hint}. Nobody thought much of it at the time, just a pile of carved stones. But the next morning, the crops withered overnight. {target_npc} says the shrine was a ward against something ancient, something that considers this land its own. The {enemy_type} have been drawn to the blighted areas like moths to flame. We need {quest_item} to rebuild the shrine and appease whatever we angered, before the settlement starves.
dialogueChunks.outro: We didn't mean to trespass on something sacred. But intentions don't matter when the ground won't feed your children.

playerResponses.text: I'll find the materials and restore the shrine. This blight ends now.
Angry spirits and cursed land? That's more than a foraging run. What's the pay?
If the land itself is cursed, no amount of gathering will fix it.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I don't believe in ghost stories. At least, I didn't until three nights ago.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The builders expanding {location} dug into an old burial site {location_hint}. Since then, lights move through the settlement at night, tools shatter without being touched, and two guards swear they saw figures standing in the fog that vanished when approached. {target_npc} consulted the oldest records and found references to a ritual that requires {quest_item} to lay the disturbed dead back to rest. The {enemy_type} in the area have become agitated too, as if the disturbance is driving them mad.
dialogueChunks.outro: The living and the dead both need this resolved. I'm asking you because everyone else is too frightened to go near those ruins.

playerResponses.text: Spirits or not, I'll put this right. Tell me what's needed.
Haunted burial grounds and angry monsters. Quite the combination. I'll do it for a price.
The dead should stay buried, and so should my involvement.

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't drink from the well, {player_name}. I'm serious. Not a single drop.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The spring that feeds {location} runs through a cavern {location_hint} that our miners breached last month. The water turned black the same day. People who drank it before we realized have fallen ill, and {target_npc} is barely keeping them alive. The miners say they saw carvings in the cavern walls, warnings in a language nobody speaks anymore. Something down there is poisoning the water deliberately, and the {enemy_type} nesting near the entrance seem to be guarding it. We need {quest_item} to purify the source, but getting there means going through whatever's protecting it.
dialogueChunks.outro: We violated something we didn't understand, and now the whole settlement is paying for it.

playerResponses.text: Contaminated water kills faster than any monster. I'll handle both.
Ancient guardians and cave monsters. This will cost you.
Sacred springs and ancient warnings? You need a priest, not a fighter.
